scenario: Arriving at a public banya and buying a session

transcript.text: Здравствуйте! Один билет в баню, пожалуйста.
transcript.romaji: Zdrávstvuyte! Odín bilyét v bányu, pazhálusta.

transcript.text: На два часа. У вас есть шапка для бани?
transcript.romaji: Na dva chasá. U vas yest' shápka dlya báni?

transcript.text: Да, конечно. И веник тоже можно купить.
transcript.romaji: Da, kanyéshna. I vénik tózhe mózhna kupít'.

translation.text: Hello! One ticket to the banya, please.
Hello! For how many hours?
For two hours. Do you have a banya hat?
Yes, of course. And you can buy a venik too.
Give me a birch venik, please.
All right. The changing room is on the right, enjoy your steam!

grammarNotes: Приятного пара is a small courtesy phrase built the same way as Приятного аппетита ('enjoy your meal') — a genitive-case wish said in passing, not a full sentence.

scenario: Asking a friend to whisk you with the venik

transcript.text: Хорошо, буду веять веником, потом легонько попарю.
transcript.romaji: Kharashó, búdu véyat' vénikom, patóm lyagón'ka papáryu.

translation.text: Igor, whisk me with the venik, please!
Okay, lie down on the bench.
Start by fanning it, don't hit hard.
Okay, I'll fan you with the venik first, then whisk you gently.
Ow, that's hot! Enduring the heat is hard!
Bear with it, it's good for your health!

grammarNotes: Попарь and попарю are forms of попарить (perfective, 'to whisk someone else'), a transitive relative of the reflexive париться ('to steam oneself') — the two verbs share a root but aren't interchangeable.

scenario: Being told the temperature and steam etiquette

transcript.text: Спросите сначала у других, не все любят сильный пар.
transcript.romaji: Sprasíte snachála u drugíkh, nye vsye lyúbyat síl'nyy par.

transcript.text: Хорошо. А сколько воды лить на камни?
transcript.romaji: Kharashó. A skól'ka vadý lit' na kámni?

transcript.text: Немного, один ковш за раз, и лучше с эфирным маслом.
transcript.romaji: Nyemnóga, odín kovsh za raz, i lúchshe s efírnym máslam.

transcript.text: А на верхнем полке очень жарко?
transcript.romaji: A na vyérkhnyem pólkye óchen' zhárka?

transcript.text: Да, там для опытных. Начните снизу.
transcript.romaji: Da, tam dlya ópytnykh. Nachníte snízu.

translation.text: May I add some steam?
Ask the others first, not everyone likes strong steam.
All right. And how much water should I pour on the stones?
A little, one ladle at a time, and it's better with essential oil.
Is it very hot on the top bench?
Yes, that's for experienced people. Start from the bottom.

grammarNotes: Poddavat' paru is a shared-room activity with real etiquette attached — asking first is the social norm, since a sudden blast of steam affects everyone in the parnaya, not just the person pouring.

transcript.text: Не бойся, окатись холодной водой — это полезно.
transcript.romaji: Nye bóysya, akatís' khalódnay vadóy — éta palyézna.

translation.text: Let's go to the ice hole, who's first?
I'm scared of the cold water!
Don't be afraid, douse yourself with cold water — it's good for you.
Fine, let's do it together!
One, two, three!
Aaah, it's cold! But it feels great!

scenario: Saying "С лёгким паром!" to a friend after their steam

translation.text: Dmitry, are you done steaming?
Yes, I stepped out to cool down.
With a light steam!
Thanks! Now it's your turn to go steam.
Okay, see you in the changing room.

grammarNotes: С лёгким паром! is fixed and near-ceremonial — it isn't built productively from other grammar, so it's best learned whole rather than analyzed word by word.

scenario: Tea and conversation after the banya

translation.text: Shall we go drink tea after the banya?
Yes, gladly. Is there any kvass?
Of course, and honey for the tea too.
Great, I'll rest for a bit now.
Rest up, banya day has only just begun!
